title: Nototriche condensata
taxonomy: Nototriche condensata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. This plant is part of the genus Nototriche, which consists of annual or perennial herbaceous plants native to South America. The classification of Nototriche condensata places it within the order Malvales, a group of flowering plants known for their diverse range of species. Further classification includes the family Malvaceae, which contains over 200 genera of plants. The specific classification of Nototriche condensata highlights its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Nototriche condensata, also known as "Condensed Nototriche" or "Puna", is a plant species native to the Andean region, belonging to the Malvaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Nototriche condensata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nototriche condensata is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and sometimes used in combination with other herbs to create a medicinal decoction or tincture.
